data.description:
Goal:
Improve OpenCode approval escalation parsing and presentation so bash permission requests show the actual command/pattern and do not appear as duplicate, indistinguishable approval cards. The observed local bun-dev case was session term-1777467128491-z9jcxs for beat knots-9aa6 in /Users/cartine/knots while another agent was running foolery-a96c in /Users/cartine/foolery. The approval records contained toolName=bash, toolUseId=functions.bash:39, patterns=["rm /Users/cartine/knots/src/db/tests_pagination.rs"], but the Approvals tab rendered only {} as the detail. There are other agents actively working in this repo; coordinate before touching shared approval UI files and do implementation work in a dedicated worktree.

Root cause notes:
1. src/lib/opencode-approval-request.ts currently treats any JSON-serializable metadata object as meaningful. renderCompact({}) returns "{}", pickRequestSummary(candidates, ["metadata"]) accepts that at lines 39-49 and 112-122, and extractOpenCodePermissionAsked then uses parameterSummary = params ?? metadata around lines 224-234 and 258-281. That lets an empty metadata object win over the useful OpenCode patterns value.
2. The useful information for this bash request was already captured in patterns, but src/lib/approval-escalations.ts formatApprovalDetailText at lines 95-109 does not consider patterns, permissionName, or toolUseId as display fallbacks, so the card shows {} instead of the rm command.
3. buildApprovalNotificationKey at src/lib/approval-escalations.ts lines 249-273 includes requestId and permissionId. The live session produced two records with the same repo, beat, terminal session, toolName, toolUseId, nativeSessionId, permissionName, and patterns, but different OpenCode permission IDs (per_dd956e814001... and per_dd956e822001...). That makes two UI rows that look identical.
4. src/components/final-cut-view.tsx lines 49-54 selects all pending approvals from the global approval store without filtering by active repo/scope. In this observation, a /Users/cartine/knots approval could be visible while the Foolery a96c session was also running, which makes the beat attribution easy to misread.

Good fix:
Normalize OpenCode permission details before they become ApprovalRequest objects. Treat empty objects/arrays as absent, explicitly derive a command display from metadata.command, command/cmd, arguments/input/toolArgs, and then patterns. Prefer a human-readable bash pattern such as rm /Users/cartine/knots/src/db/tests_pagination.rs over raw JSON. Extend formatApprovalDetailText to fall back to patterns and permission/tool identity when no message/question/toolParamsDisplay/parameterSummary exists. Add a logical dedupe key for display/notification coalescing that can collapse repeated OpenCode permission.asked events with identical repo, beat, terminal session, native session, toolUseId, permissionName, and patterns even if native permissionId differs; preserve the latest native reply target or explicitly represent multiple reply targets so approving still reaches OpenCode safely. Make the Approvals tab repo-aware, or visibly label cross-repo approvals, so an approval from /Users/cartine/knots cannot be mistaken for foolery-a96c when /Users/cartine/foolery is the active repo.

Verification:
1. Add a unit fixture matching the observed OpenCode bash rm permission request with empty metadata and patterns=["rm /Users/cartine/knots/src/db/tests_pagination.rs"]. Verify extractOpenCodePermissionAsked returns parameterSummary or toolParamsDisplay containing the rm command, not {}.
2. Verify formatApprovalDetailText renders the rm command/pattern for that approval card.
3. Add a duplicate/coalescing test with two OpenCode permission.asked events that differ only by requestId/permissionId and verify the UI/store does not show two indistinguishable pending rows.
4. Add or update FinalCut/Approvals tab coverage proving approvals are scoped to the active repo, or that cross-repo rows show an explicit repo label and cannot be mistaken for the active Foolery beat.
5. Run focused approval tests, then bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test, and bun run build before shipping.

Constraints:
Do not start implementation from the dirty main checkout. The current checkout already has unrelated edits in src/components/final-cut-view.tsx, src/components/__tests__/final-cut-view-tabs.test.ts, and untracked worktree-foolery-a96c/. Coordinate with the active agents before editing final-cut-view.tsx or approval store/UI files. Keep tests hermetic; do not depend on the live local bun-dev session or host OpenCode process.
